label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about when the telephone booth was fitted. The story mentions that the man from the telephone department got off the bus and went to the tea stall. Then it says, "It was a year ago that the phone line had been installed, six months later men from the public works department had come to put up the phone booth." So, the phone line was installed a year ago, then six months after that, the phone booth was put up. Let me break that down. If the phone line was installed a year ago, that's 12 months. Then the phone booth was added six months after that, so 12 + 6 = 18 months total. But the question is about the process of fitting a working telephone booth. Wait, the phone line was installed first, then the booth. So the booth was installed six months after the phone line, which was a year ago. So the booth was put up a year ago plus six months, so 18 months ago? Or is the phone line being installed a year ago, and the booth six months later, meaning that the booth was installed six months after the phone line. So the process of setting up the booth wasn't a year, but part of that timeline. The question is asking which of the options is correct. Let's look at the options again: (A) was a process that had already taken 12 months (B) was in the charge of the headman. (C) should have been finished more than a year ago (D) was an artistic challenge for a local painter The story says the phone line was installed a year ago, then the booth six months later. But the telephone booth itself was put up six months after the line. The painter came after the booth was installed to paint the sign. The painter took a day, but the question is about the process of fitting the booth. The headman is mentioned later when the telephone man gives him the number, but it's not stated that he was in charge of the process. The painter's part is
